observations.recommendation:
We recommended and that the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the members of the Inventory Committee to completely conduct the physical count of all the municipality’s PPEs and prepare the RPCPPE for submission to the Audit Team, not later than January 31 of each year.
We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed, through the GSO-Designate, to always update the property cards of all PPEs and perform periodic reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Accountant to allow timely detection of errors and necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended and that Management agreed to require the concerned departments responsible in the recording the road network of the Municipality to coordinate and establish a system to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Further, we recommended and that Management agreed to inquire from the DILG Regional Office VII for the training schedules on LRAMS, and allow the concerned officials to attend, if available.

We recommended and that Management, through the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate, agreed to reconcile the balances of land account reflected in their respective records, to present fairly the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to record in the books of accounts the audit disallowances that have become final and executory as contained in the NFDs; and to require the persons liable to settle/pay immediately them to the agency cashier.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to reconcile the noted discrepancy in the above-mentioned reciprocal accounts and thereafter, effect the necessary adjusting entries thereon to correct their ending balances.

We recommended and that Management, through the coordination of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, agreed to continue reconciling the cashbook balances with the accounting records for the CIB-LCCA and Cash in Local Treasury accounts. This is to resolve the discrepancies, effect the necessary adjustments and consistently harmonize the balances of both records.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to implement the following:
Prepare an adjusting entry to record the current year’s unspent 30 per cent QRF and 70 per cent MF-MOOE of LDRRMF totaling ₱5,722,190.47 from GF to the TF; and
Revert to the GF the unexpended balance of LDRRMF that is already beyond the five- year period totaling ₱320,402.66 to make it available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to draw journal entry vouchers to transfer the above completed projects and purchased equipment from the TF to the GF books in accordance with Section 104, 1(i) of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Thereafter, provide the necessary depreciation for these asset accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the following proposed adjusting journal entries in both the TF and the GF books, and thereafter, compute and record the necessary accumulated depreciation of these assets:
We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to facilitate the return of the remaining ₱103.03 to fully liquidate and close the “Due to NGAS” account in the Trust Fund book.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the OIC-HRMO, agreed to prioritize the filling in of the vacant statutory/mandatory positions in the Municipality’s DRRM Office as required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 and Rule 6 Section 2 of the IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the charging against the LDRRM funds expenditures related to maintenance or operation of the LDRRM Office to ensure that the funds are allocated exclusively for risk and disaster-related programs and activities of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Engineer, agreed to comply with the full disclosure requirement in the submission of quarterly report of government PPAs to promote an honest, transparent, and orderly management of public funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer agreed to implement the following:
Fast track the submission of contracts with complete set of supporting documents to facilitate the immediate conduct of the auditorial and technical review of the projects; and
For completed projects, submit the complete set of documents required in the same COA circular for technical inspection.

We recommended and the BAC, through the BAC Secretariat agreed to implement the following:
Comply with GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 in the submission of the APP, PMR and APCPI Results to the GPPB using the above provided email addresses;
Post the duly approved PMR in the agency’s website; and
Coordinate with the personnel assigned in the maintenance of the agency’s website to resolve the issue on the non-posting of APPs.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the General Services Officer, agreed to prioritize the registration, transfer, and titling of these parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Dumanjug to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386. As an alternative, we recommended for the creation of Land Titling Committee to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to develop and implement policy guidelines to standardize wage rates for the JO workers of the Municipality to ensure that JOs receive fair and equitable compensation in line with their assigned tasks

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Engineer, agreed to submit a detailed explanation on each item with noted deficiency, otherwise, require the contractor to settle or rectify all the deficiencies noted and thereafter, submit a report for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

We recommended and the Management through the GFPS agreed to implement the following measures:
Prepare the three-year GAD Agenda in order for the Municipality to have its strategic framework and plan for gender mainstreaming and achieving women empowerment and gender equality; and
Henceforth, review the alignment of the annual GPB with the GAD Agenda.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the following measures:
Cause the reconciliation of the recorded mandatory contributions withheld and payments on loan amortization against the actual remittances and payments to identify the breakdown of the balances and cause the immediate remittance or payment; and
Conduct monthly reconciliation for the Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts to prevent unnecessary accumulation of unremitted/ unpaid balances.

Management’s Comment:


I. Item No. 200 (Aggregate sub-base Coarse)


  The aggregate sub-base coarse used in this  project  is  white  limestone
  known as giso. It is unfortunate that during the period of  this  project
  that the Provincial Government  of  Cebu  suspended  quarry  permits  and
  stopped processing all quarry permit applications (wherein only less than
  5% of quarries in Cebu were operational).


  The price of giso  was  affected  as  prices  went  up  from  ₱900.00  to
  ₱1,200.00 due to the effect of increased demand and low supply.


II. Item No. 311 (Portland Cement Concrete Pavement, 200mm thk.)
  The management relied on the same  cost  on  similar  projects  from  the
  previous year there being no COA observations.


  The said project is now being enjoyed by the general public and benefited
  by the same.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The Audit Team will forward the written  explanation  to  the  COA  Regional
Technical  Services  Office  for  them  to  assess   the   merit   of   such
justification.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant
to record the inventories and the corresponding liability upon  delivery  of
the goods received.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to  strictly  follow
the procedures and proper recording as  provided  under  Chapter  7  of  the
Manual on NGAS.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant  to
transfer the unexpended balance of the  DRRM  funds  to  the  unappropriated
surplus of the  General  Fund  after  the  validity  period  of  five  years
pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that  Management  remit  the  tax  deficiency  or  the  under
withheld amount to the BIR. Any penalty or interest arising  therefrom  must
be shouldered personally by the responsible personnel. We  also  recommended
that Management comply with the correct withholding of tax for each  payment
made to its supplier or contractor pursuant to Section  2.57.2  (J)  of  the
Bureau of Internal Revenue RR No. 2-98 as renumbered and amended by  RR  11-
2018.

Management’s Comment:


It is in good faith that the  Agency  considered  the  payments  to  various
Infra-assets to its suppliers as procurement of goods, that is why  only  1%
is withheld and remitted to BIR.  The  Management  henceforth  undertake  to
comply with the COA observation as to the correct  withholding  of  tax  for
each payment made to its supplier of services.

However,  the  Management  dissent  from  the  recommendation  as   to   the
remittance of under withheld tax  equivalent  to  1%  of  the  total  income
payments made by the Agency as the remittance of an additional 1% tax  would
create unjust enrichment to BIR. Regardless of amount of tax  withheld,  the
total income tax due of the supplier will not change, only  the  net  amount
of cash to be remitted by the supplier to BIR.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The Audit Team will keep from the audit recommendation as to the  remittance
of tax deficiency or the under withheld amount to the BIR.  The  Audit  Team
will prospectively monitor the application of this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to reconcile their balances. Further, instruct the  Municipal
   Treasurer/Property  Officer  and  other  Section  Chiefs  to  coordinate
   closely with the Municipal Accountant regarding  the  nature  and  other
   details of  various  property  and  equipment  used  by  the  respective
   offices.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to immediately draw journal entry vouchers upon submission of
   the supporting documents to record the  reconciling  item  in  order  to
   reflect the correct cash in bank and PPE account balances.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to determine the  causes  of  the  negative  balances  of  the
   accounts and submit explanation  and  prioritize  the  reconciliation  of
   accounts to  ensure  that  the  accounts  are  fairly  presented  in  the
   financial statements.

We  recommended  that  Municipal  Mayor  instruct   the   Municipal
   Accountant to: (a) reclassify all semi-expandable properties which  were
   recognized as PPE to the appropriate accounts and (b) apply the rules on
   capitalization threshold  of  ₱10,000  for  PPE  as  prescribed  in  COA
   Circular  No. 97-005.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to  determine  the
   acquisition cost, appraised value, or assessed value reflected in the Tax
   Declaration of  the  land  where  the  various  municipal  buildings  was
   constructed as basis in recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
   Engineer to see to it that the conduct of detailed engineering in various
   infrastructure projects shall always be carried out  in  accordance  with
   the provisions of RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR.

        We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer immediately  comply
   with the requirements of the Technical Inspection and Technology Services
   (TITS) office in order to facilitate contract review and  for  the  audit
   team to assess the propriety and reasonableness of the amount paid.

We  recommended  that  the  cash  advances  of  the  LGU  shall  be
   liquidated in  accordance  with  the  provisions  of  the  aforementioned
   circular.  The  concerned  officers/employees  with   unliquidated   cash
   advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same in order to  avoid
   the sanctions imposed under existing laws  and  regulations  specifically
   under Article 218 of  the  Revised  Penal  Code  for  failure  to  render
   account.

        We also recommended that demand letters be sent immediately by  the
   Municipal Accountant to the accountable officers  especially  those  that
   are no longer connected with the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health
   Officer to immediately comply and install the (one (1) unit compact table
   top radiography with work station (Fujifilm FCR Prima T2)  to  facilitate
   the inspection by the COA TITS office and for the audit team to ascertain
   the reasonableness of the amount paid to  avoid  disallowance  in  audit.
   Further, the Management shall immediately  hire  a  qualified  person  to
   operate the machine once it is installed.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  BAC  and
   Municipal  Accountant   to   submit   the   required   documents   and/or
   justification why no failure of bidding was declared for  our  evaluation
   to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of  cash  advances  within
   the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and must see
   to it that no additional  cash  advances  shall  be  allowed  unless  the
   previous cash advance is first settled or proper  accounting  thereof  is
   made.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Engineer and other  officials  concerned  to  explain  their  failure  to
   monitor the on time delivery of the materials purchased;  and  liquidated
   damages be computed,  imposed  and  collected  on  the  payment  for  the
   delivery beyond the expiry date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) Management should coordinate with the proper government agencies to secure the titling of the lots in the name of the LGU and undertake an appraisal of these properties so that their appraised values can be recorded in the books of accounts.
b) The Municipal Accountant should review and compute in accordance with the guidelines in the NGAS Manual, the correct balance of the Accumulated Depreciation that should have been recorded in the books, taking into consideration the dates of acquisition and estimated useful lives of the assets and book the proper adjustments accordingly.
c) The Property Officer should prepare the Inventory & Inspection Report of all identified unserviceable properties and facilitate their immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values. The Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE account to transfer the book value of unserviceable properties to Other Assets account, pending disposal, and close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account if any was provided.
d) The Accounting and Property Sections should reconcile their records on PPE and investigate the causes of the discrepancies so that these can be disposed of accordingly. Henceforth, this activity should be performed on a regular basis.
e) The municipal accountant should coordinate with the engineering department and secure the necessary documents to support the journal entry voucher which should be drawn to reclassify the assets to the proper PPE account; likewise compute and make the necessary adjustments for the correct depreciation charges that should be provided.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 Audit recommendations b and c were already implemented.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit a report of physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
a) Management should coordinate with the proper government agencies to secure the titling of the lots in the name of the LGU and undertake an ap-praisal of these properties so that their appraised values can be recorded in the books of accounts.
b) The Municipal Accountant should review and compute in accordance with the guidelines in the NGAS Manual, the correct balance of the Accumulated Depreciation that should have been recorded in the books, taking into consideration the dates of acquisition and estimated useful lives of the assets and book the proper adjustments accordingly.
c) The Property Officer should prepare the Inventory & Inspection Report of all identified unserviceable properties and facilitate their immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values. The Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE account to transfer the book value of unserviceable properties to Other Assets account, pending disposal, and close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account if any was provided.
d) The Accounting and Property Sections should reconcile their records on PPE and investigate the causes of the discrepancies so that these can be disposed of accordingly. Henceforth, this activity should be performed on a regular basis.
e) The municipal accountant should coordinate with the engineering depart-ment and secure the necessary documents to support the journal entry voucher which should be drawn to reclassify the assets to the proper PPE account; likewise compute and make the necessary adjustments for the correct depreciation charges that should be provided.
